activity_metadata.objective: Students review key points from "Teenagers" by Pat Mora to show literal comprehension of the text.
activity_metadata.alignment_long: This activity replaces the Quiz and Think question segment of the Teenagers First Read activity. It offers a scaffolded sequence that guides students through demonstrating comprehension by responding to questions using evidence from the text.
activity_metadata.excerpt:
Teenagers by Pat Mora

(1) One day they disappear

       into their rooms.

       Doors and lips shut

       and we become strangers

(5) in our own home.

       I pace the hall, hear whispers,

       a code I knew but can't remember,

       mouthed by mouths I taught to speak.

       Years later the door opens.

(10) I see faces I once held,

       open as sunflowers in my hands. I see

       familiar skin now stretched on long bodies

       that move past me

       glowing almost like pearls.
exit_ticket: Write two or three sentences that explain what happens to the “strangers” when “years later, the door opens”?

questions_map.question:
**Voice** is the way an author uses word choice, tone, and speech patterns to show the personality of a speaker, narrator, or character. Voice gives the sense that a real person is talking to the reader or to other characters. 

Whose voice is telling us about the teenagers?
In lines 4 and 5, the speaker says that ”we become strangers in our own home.” Who are the strangers in this home?
Re-read lines 6-8. Which of the following inferences is best supported by these lines?
Look at lines 6-8 again. What does the word “code” mean in this context?
Re-read lines 9-14. These lines primarily suggest that _____.
Write two or three sentences that explain what happens to the “strangers” when “years later, the door opens”?

USER_5.question: In lines 4 and 5, the speaker says that ”we become strangers in our own home.” Who are the strangers in this home?

responses.content: La voz que nos hablan sobre Los  adolesentes comopuede ser la de un narrador en primera persona (un adolescente mismo), que comparte sus experiencias directamente, o la de un narrador en tercera persona (omnisciente o limitado), que observa y describe a los adolescentes desde afuera, a menudo con un tono que revela la perspectiva del autor (nostálgico, crítico, empático), o incluso la voz de un personaje adulto que los describe, creando una distancia. En esencia, es la voz única del personaje o narrador que el autor elige para transmitir la personalidad y mundo adolesente.
Su madre

USER_5.question: Re-read lines 6-8. Which of the following inferences is best supported by these lines?

responses.content: Los adolesentes Que estan grades o cresieron
Su mama
Los hermanos

USER_5.question: Re-read lines 9-14. These lines primarily suggest that _____.

responses.content: La voz que nos habla sobre adolescentes en las líneas 6-8 es probablemente la de un narrador en primera persona (un adolescente) o un narrador joven en tercera persona, que usa jerga y observa sus comportamientos, y la palabra "código" se refiere a un conjunto de reglas o un lenguaje secreto y no escrito que usan los adolescentes para comunicarse y entenderse entre ellos, mostrando su pertenencia a un grupo y diferenciandose de adultos.

USER_26.question: Write two or three sentences that explain what happens to the “strangers” when “years later, the door opens”?

responses.content: After the door opens, the parents see faces of their teenagers that were once merely babies. They see babies they once held in their hands. Also, familiar skin which is now much longer and glowing radiantly like pearls.
They are now fully grown teenagers aged fourteen to sixteen.
